Details:
The use of ozone in combination with hydrogen peroxide resulted in mixed treatment efficiencies, depending on the dose of ozone and H2O2 employed. One bench-scale study conducted on surface and ground water achieved 78 to 90 percent removal with an ozone dose of 2 mg/L and an H2O2 dose of 0.7 mg/L.
Another bench-scale study found removals of 50 and 30 percent with spiked lab and surface water, respectively, with an ozone dose of 1 mg/L and an H2O2 dose of 0.4 mg/L. However, when the dose of ozone and H2O2 were increased to 3.7 and 1.4 mg/L, 93 percent removal was achieved. Even greater removal of the contaminant, over 99 percent, was obtained with an ozone dose of 5.0 mg/L and a hydrogen peroxide dose of 1.8 mg/L.
July 2009
